response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r1c0 is in acute survival crisis: islanded with SoC at 10% (0.20/2 kWh) and 1.00 kW load, yielding ~12 minutes to complete depletion. The past hour reveals a systematic rejection wall—all three potential peers (r0c0, r1c1, r2c0) have refused outbound transfers, citing r1c0's low SoC, distrusted status, and their own emergency preservation modes. The previous conservative 60% threshold policy has proven counterproductive, isolating r1c0 precisely when mutual aid is most critical. Peers are themselves in freefall (r0c0 critically low, r1c1 at 13 min to depletion, r2c0 guarding 50% threshold), suggesting network-wide islanding stress. Immediate action requires: (1) aggressive load reduction to extend survival window and signal good-faith reserve preservation, (2) pivot to inbound-only posture to accept emergency aid without triggering peer distrust, (3) escalate to aggregator/grid coordination for emergency supply, and (4) temporarily suspend outbound sharing requests to rebuild peer confidence. The belief that balanced sharing with 15% minimum reserve will enable mutual aid is invalidated by current peer rejection patterns—trust must be rebuilt through demonstrated self-preservation discipline first.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.30
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "CRITICAL SURVIVAL MODE: SoC 10%, ~12 min to depletion. Suspend all outbound sharing requests immediately. Shift to inbound-only posture to accept emergency aid. Load reduction to 0.3–0.5 kW strongly recommended to extend survival window and signal reserve discipline. Previous peer rejections indicate network-wide emergency; mutual aid impossible until r1c0 demonstrates self-preservation. Escalate to aggregator for emergency grid coordination. Rebuild trust through demonstrated restraint before resuming balanced sharing."
ttl_ticks: 1
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
